concerns.text: FR-002 mandates downloading 'merger rate data' from IllustrisTNG (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.3566863) to compare against GWTC mass/spin distributions. The Assumptions section explicitly admits this TNG release 'does NOT contain resolved component mass and spin distributions.' You cannot validate a distributional hypothesis (mass_ratio, effective_spin) using a dataset that lacks those variables. This is a category error: the validation target (TNG rates) is not the same physical quantity as the predictor (TNG mass/spin distributions), rendering the proposed KS test in FR-005 impossible to execute as described.

The core validation logic (KS test) requires two distributions of the same variable. The spec proposes comparing GWTC mass/spin posteriors against IllustrisTNG 'merger rate data.' Merger rates (events/Gpc^3/yr) are scalar integrals over the mass/spin space, not distributions. Comparing a distribution (GWTC) to a scalar rate (TNG) via a Kolmogorov-Smirnov test is mathematically invalid. The spec fails to define the 'Simulation_Dataset' distribution required for the test.

FR-009 requires a sensitivity analysis sweeping alpha thresholds {0.04, 0.05, 0.06}. While statistically valid in isolation, this is a trivial post-hoc check that does not address the fundamental data mismatch identified in FR-002/FR-005. If the primary comparison is invalid (distribution vs. scalar), the sensitivity analysis of the significance threshold is meaningless. The methodology focuses on tuning the test rather than fixing the data source.
